text: Mobile app development involves creating software for smartphones and tablets. A key principle is responsive design, which ensures the app adapts smoothly to different screen sizes and orientations. Developers often choose between native and cross-platform development. Native apps are built for a specific system, like iOS or Android, offering high performance, while cross-platform tools allow a single codebase to run on multiple platforms, saving time and cost. Once built, apps are published on app stores like Google Play or Apple's App Store, where users download them. Performance is critical—slow or buggy apps frustrate users and get poor reviews. A well-designed app balances functionality with speed.
